cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the correct answer. The question is about Wu Jinyou and which statement is true regarding him. The options are A to D. First, I'll need to refer back to the given text to find any mentions of Wu Jinyou. Let me scan through the passage again. The article mentions Wu Jinyou in the part that says: "Wu Jinyou, head of the Polar Research Institute of China, and the group's leader, said, 'I hope the students have not only a field trip to the Yellow River station, but are also touched by the devotion and spirit of the Chinese researchers here. I also expect in the future, some of them will choose to devote themselves to polar research and maybe even work with us.'" So from this, we know that Wu Jinyou is the head of the Polar Research Institute of China and the leader of the group. His statement includes that he hopes the students are touched by the devotion and spirit of the researchers and that maybe some will pursue polar research. Now let's look at the options again: A) He is a graduate from Dalian Marinetime University. B) He is the president of Dalian Marinetime University. C) He thinks highly of the devotion and spirit of the Chinese researchers. D) He is sure that all the ten students will make good polar researchers. Looking at the text, there's no mention that Wu Jinyou is a graduate of Dalian Marinetime University (A), nor is he mentioned as the president of that university (B). That's about Zheng Li, one of the students, who is a major in telecommunications at Dalian Marinetime University. So A and B are incorrect. Option C says he thinks highly of the devotion and spirit of the Chinese researchers. In his quote, he says he hopes the students are "touched by the devotion and spirit of the Chinese researchers here." This implies that he himself appreciates or values their devotion and spirit. So C seems correct. Option D states that he is sure all ten students will become good polar researchers.
